nuggets.headline: Radiohead briefly replaced their long-time producer during the making of *In Rainbows*
nuggets.text: Before finally settling back in with Nigel Godrich for *In Rainbows*, Radiohead actually tried working with another producer, Mark Stent, in early 2006. Bassist Colin Greenwood explained the decision by saying that the relationship with Godrich had "got a little too safe," hinting at the band's constant push for new directions. However, these sessions didn't yield much, and they returned to Godrich later that year, who ultimately produced the album.

source.title: In Rainbows - Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia
source.quoteSnippet: In the February 2006 sessions, they chose to work with producer Mark Stent instead of their longtime co-producer Nigel Godrich. Bassist Colin Greenwood, commenting on their decision, said 'Nigel and the band know each other so well now, it's all got a little too safe.'

nuggets.imageCaption: Mark Stent, producer Radiohead briefly worked with for 'In Rainbows'.

nuggets.headline: Radiohead's *In Rainbows* release strategy caused "all hell to break loose" in the music industry
nuggets.text: The release of *In Rainbows* wasn't just about the music; it was a revolution. On September 30, 2007, guitarist Jonny Greenwood casually announced on their blog that the album was finished and would be available in 10 days, allowing fans to pay "how much, or how little, they wanted" for the digital download. This unprecedented "pay-what-you-want" model, enacted without their previous record label EMI, sent shockwaves through the entire record business and was described by one source as ripping through EMI's office "like a dirty bomb."

source.title: Chasing rainbows: inside the battle between Radiohead and EMI's Guy Hands | Radiohead | The Guardian
source.quoteSnippet: On Sunday 30 September 2007, just hours before the calendar flipped into Q4... Radiohead guitarist Jonny Greenwood posted a message... "Well, the new album is finished, and it’s coming out in 10 days. We’ve called it In Rainbows... In Rainbows would be available, with no record company involvement... and fans could choose how much, or how little, they wanted to pay for the download version... When it became clear what precisely they were doing, all hell broke loose – not just within EMI but across the entire record business.

nuggets.imageCaption: Jonny Greenwood casually announced on their blog that the album

nuggets.headline: hear Radiohead's producer build songs from "thin air" in his other band, Ultraísta
nuggets.text: If you're looking to dive deeper into the creative world of Radiohead's long-time producer, Nigel Godrich, check out his band Ultraísta. Alongside multi-instrumentalist Joey Waronker and vocalist Laura Bettinson, Godrich crafts a unique sound. They sometimes take an unconventional approach to songwriting, with Bettinson singing melodies to "thin air" and the music being added later, creating a distinct, genre-bending experience.

source.title: Radiohead producer, Nigel Godrich debuts Ultraista LP.
source.quoteSnippet: “It’s almost like remixing,” Godrich says. “Sometimes Laura would sing melodies to thin air, and we’d add the music later.”

nuggets.imageCaption: Laura Bettinson and Nigel Godrich of Ultraísta.

artistSummary: Radiohead, the pioneering English alternative rock band, reshaped the music industry with their innovative sound and groundbreaking release strategies. Known for their experimental approach, they consistently challenge musical norms and deeply engage their listeners.
